paper_title: Elemental Analysis on Marine Sediments Related to Depositional Environment of Bangka Strait

methods.description: Collection and selection of gravity core samples from the western part of Bangka Strait for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: This study was conducted on four selected gravity core samples (MBB-67, MBB-119, MBB-120 and MBB-173) that have been collected in 1996 from western part of Bangka Strait, between 9 -30 m water depths.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect gravity core samples from specified marine location and depth range.

steps.description: Selection of four specific gravity core samples (MBB-67, MBB-119, MBB-120 and MBB-173) for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific core samples from collected set.
outputs: selected gravity core samples (MBB-67, MBB-119, MBB-120, MBB-173)

methods.description: Non-destructive scanning of selected gravity core samples using MSCL-S Geotek to measure magnetic susceptibility and elemental composition (XRF) at regular intervals.

steps.description: Prepare selected core samples for scanning. (Implicit step based on using a scanner).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare core samples for non-destructive scanning.

steps.description: Scan core samples using MSCL-S Geotek, at the Core Laboratory of the Marine Geological Institute in Cirebon.
steps.searchable_summary: Scan prepared core samples using MSCL-S Geotek.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility (MS) measurement was performed using Bartington MS2E point sensor... Both sensors are installed to the MSCL equipment.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure magnetic susceptibility using Bartington MS2E sensor during scanning.

steps.description: ...elemental composition of sediment was measured by XRF handheld Olympus Innov-X. Both sensors are installed to the MSCL equipment.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure elemental composition using XRF handheld Olympus Innov-X sensor during scanning.

steps.description: These samples were analyzed by a nondestructive scanning method in order to determine magnetic susceptibility and X-Ray Fluorescence in 2 cm intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Record measurements at 2 cm intervals along the core.

methods.description: Analysis of elemental correlation coefficients to determine the most suitable elemental ratios for use as grain-size and REE content proxies.

steps.description: Obtain elemental concentration data (Zr, Ti, Rb, Y) from XRF measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract relevant elemental concentration data (Zr, Ti, Rb, Y) from XRF results.

steps.description: Calculations of correlation coefficient on Zr, Ti and Rb (Table 1) yield highest value on Zr/Ti (0.7) that is used as grain-size proxy in this paper. This selection is supported by its high correlation coefficient (0.61) as is presented in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate correlation coefficients between specified elements (Zr, Ti, Rb, Y).
outputs: correlation coefficients (e.g., corr(Zr, Ti), corr(Zr, Rb), corr(Y, Zr))

steps.description: Evaluate correlation coefficients to select the best elemental ratios as proxies (Zr/Ti for grain size, Y/Zr for REE).
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate correlation results to select optimal elemental ratios for proxies.

methods.description: Calculation of specific geochemical proxy values (Zr/Ti ratio for grain size, ln(Y/Zr) ratio for REE content) from elemental concentration data.

steps.description: Obtain elemental concentration data (Zr, Ti, Y) from XRF measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract relevant elemental concentration data (Zr, Ti, Y) from XRF results.

steps.description: Calculations of correlation coefficient on Zr, Ti and Rb (Table 1) yield highest value on Zr/Ti (0.7) that is used as grain-size proxy in this paper.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Zr/Ti ratio for each measurement interval.

steps.description: Yttrium concentration in study area is presented as natural log ratio (ln) to avoid variation related to sedimentary dilution effect (Dypvik and Harris (2001). This study uses log ratio of Y/Zr as proxy of REE content.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the natural log ratio of Y/Zr (ln(Y/Zr)) for each measurement interval.
